title: lippia fastigiata
intro: Lippia fastigiata, also known as Mexican lippia or Mexican oregano, is a perennial shrub native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and aromatic leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Lippia fastigiata include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems, as well as its potential in lowering cholesterol levels and blood pressure. Lippia fastigiata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a spice to add flavor and aroma to various dishes.
